title: P-023: terminal-state --reopen rule for blackboard.py (gen-5 backlog, LOW)
description: From evolution.next_audit_inputs: failed/done are terminal today; define and implement an explicit --reopen verb with logging (who, why), refusing silent resurrection of terminal tasks. Small, guarded change to blackboard.py + ORCHESTRATION.md note.

notes.note: plan: add explicit 'reopen' verb to blackboard.py (terminal-state only, mandatory --note, task_reopened event, resets to open clearing claim/handoff) + document in ORCHESTRATION.md lifecycle section; test end-to-end on a scratch failed task

EXPECTED: reopen verb refuses non-terminal + no-note, succeeds on terminal+note, logs task_reopened, clears claim/handoff/completion. ACTUAL: all confirmed via scratch T-304 (add-task -> claim -> failed -> reopen w/o note refused exit 1, status unchanged 'failed' -> reopen w/ note succeeded exit 0, status->open, claimed_by/claim_expires_at/handoff/completed_at/completed_by all null, task_reopened event logged with agent+previous_status+note in events.jsonl, REOPEN note appended to detail file -> re-attempted reopen on now-open T-304 correctly refused as not-terminal. T-304 driven back to failed as disposable artifact.

HANDOFF -> verifier: P-023 reopen verb implemented in .harness/bin/blackboard.py (cmd_reopen + 'reopen' subparser, house style: hc.guarded(), resolve_note/append_note, hc.log_event, atomic save_bb, expire_claims). ORCHESTRATION.md lifecycle section (section 2) documents it. REPLAY (all commands run verbatim, exit codes as noted): (1) python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py add-task --id T-304 --title 'SCRATCH-P023: reopen verb probe (disposable)' --role worker --engine any --priority 9 --description '...' --agent worker-b -> added. (2) claim T-304 --agent worker-b -> claimed. (3) update T-304 --status failed --agent worker-b --note '...' -> status=failed. (4) reopen T-304 --agent worker-b (NO --note) -> exit 1, stderr 'refused: reopen requires --note ... no note means no reopen (P-023: refuses silent resurrection)'; verified via blackboard.json that status stayed 'failed' (no mutation). (5) reopen T-304 --agent worker-b --note '...' -> exit 0, prints "reopened T-304 (was 'failed') -> 'open' by worker-b..."; blackboard.json shows status=open, claimed_by=null, claim_expires_at=null, handoff=null, completed_at=null, completed_by=null. (6) grep task_reopened .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> {"event": "task_reopened", "agent": "worker-b", "task": "T-304", "previous_status": "failed", "note": "..."}. (7) T-304.json notes[] shows the REOPEN-prefixed note appended. (8) reopen T-304 --agent worker-b --note '...' AGAIN (T-304 now 'open') -> exit 1, refused as non-terminal ('reopen only acts on done or failed'), confirming the guard is status-based not one-shot. T-304 was then re-driven to 'failed' and left as a disposable scratch artifact. All 5 acceptance points verified: (1) terminal-only gate, (2) mandatory --agent (via shared parent parser, consistent with every other verb) + mandatory --note (hard refusal, checked before any state mutation), (3) task_reopened event with who/why, (4) silent resurrection refused, (5) clean reset to open clearing claim/handoff/completion. Verifier: re-run the 8 replay commands above (or your own scratch task) to confirm.

VERIFIED (verifier-b, rotation from verifier-a/T-300 since this is a control-plane change): read cmd_reopen (blackboard.py L470-520) -- follows house conventions: hc.guarded() wraps the mutating block, load_bb/save_bb atomic pair, resolve_note() for P-021 --note/--note-file/--note-stdin, hc.log_event('task_reopened', agent, previous_status, note) after save, expire_claims()+report_expirations() called consistently with other verbs. Static read found no holes: terminal-only gate (status in ('done','failed') else sys.exit before any mutation), --agent resolves via shared 'common' parser default=hc.agent_id() like every other verb, full state clear (claimed_by/claim_expires_at/handoff/completed_at/completed_by all set None, mirrors cmd_handoff's clearing pattern), cascade gate is untouched by this verb (claim's own depends_on check re-runs on next claim, not memoized). REPLAYED MYSELF on fresh scratch T-305 (not trusting worker-b's T-304 log): (1) add-task/claim/update->failed; (2) reopen w/o --note -> exit 1, stderr 'refused: reopen requires --note...', verified via md5 of blackboard.json unchanged (75ff3e05a02a10fd4b3ea0dc24f704f3 before and after) -- zero mutation confirmed cryptographically, not just by eyeballing status; (3) reopen --note '' (empty string, an edge worker-b did not test) -> also refused cleanly, exit 1, no traceback, no mutation; (4) reopen T-999 (nonexistent task, another edge worker-b did not test) -> 'unknown task T-999', exit 1, clean, no mutation; (5) reopen w/ real --note -> exit 0, status->open, all 5 fields (claimed_by/claim_expires_at/handoff/completed_at/completed_by) confirmed None via direct JSON read, task_reopened event in events.jsonl carries agent=verifier-b/previous_status=failed/note=<mine>, REOPEN-prefixed note appended to T-305.json; (6) re-attempted reopen on now-open T-305 -> refused as non-terminal, clean message, not a traceback. COGNITIVE-DIVERSITY CHECK beyond worker-b's method: built a second scratch task T-306 with depends_on=T-305 to prove claim (4) of the docstring/ORCHESTRATION.md -- 'cascade gate re-evaluates depends_on normally on the next claim' -- is true, not just claimed: drove T-305 through claim->handoff->update-done (cascade unlocked T-306, claim succeeded), then reopened T-305 done->open, and re-attempted claim T-306 -> correctly REFUSED again ('depends on unfinished task(s): T-305'), proving the gate is live-evaluated each claim and reopen does not leave a stale unlocked state. ORCHESTRATION.md L58-67 documents exactly the 4 behaviors implemented, no overclaim, matches code and my replay verbatim (including the cascade-regate claim I independently verified). Scratch cleanup: T-305 and T-306 driven back to failed (disposable artifacts, same convention as worker-b's T-304). Housekeeping: lock.py status/sweep run, 0 expired locks swept, no active locks outstanding at verdict time.
